reasoning: The Lean `f` definition uses `eval₂RingHom (aeval (a•Y + C p)).toRingHom (C X)` on `ℂ[X][Y]`, which sends `Y ↦ C X` (i.e., y ↦ x) and `C X ↦ a•Y + C p` (i.e., x ↦ p(x) + ay), exactly matching the informal map. The hypotheses `p.natDegree > 1` is equivalent to "degree > 1" (they agree for both zero and nonzero polynomials), `a ≠ 0`, `𝔭.IsPrime`, and `𝔭.height = 1` (which for a prime ideal equals `primeHeight`, the standard height via chains of primes) all match the informal. `𝔭.map (f a p)` is the ideal generated by the image, which coincides with the direct image since f is an automorphism when a ≠ 0 (invertible via x ↦ y, y ↦ (x - p(y))/a). The type `ℂ[X][Y]` is canonically isomorphic to `MvPolynomial (Fin 2) ℂ` representing ℂ[x,y]. The duplicate parameter name `h` is merely cosmetic shadowing — both hypotheses remain part of the statement.
